research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
quote_text_short: “no-one else should have to go through what I did”
quote_context: Public patient quote in source; context: metastatic breast cancer presenting as back pain; stage: repeat visit; medical history; specialist review; emergency escalation
topic_category: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored
pain_point_or_theme: No one clinician/system connected the repeated back-pain presentations with prior cancer risk.
why_it_matters: Surface unresolved symptom threads, prior high-risk history and multiple-clinician patterns in the EHR.
notes: Named public case from national safety investigation; includes patient voice.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
quote_text_short: “I was constantly dismissed, ignored and belittled”
quote_context: Public patient quote in source; context: endometriosis; stage: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit
topic_category: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required
pain_point_or_theme: Chronic pain and women's health symptoms can be minimized despite repeated presentations.
why_it_matters: Track symptom duration/severity, repeated visits and invalidating language; prompt specialist referral criteria review.
notes: Advocacy survey gives patient voice and quantitative delay data.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
quote_context: Public patient quote in source; context: varied unresolved conditions while waiting; stage: referral; follow-up; lab interpretation
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; patient self-advocacy required
pain_point_or_theme: Referral is not a completed action unless the patient is seen, tests are done and results acted on.
why_it_matters: Track referral state, wait risk, abnormal-test escalation and patient-visible next step.
notes: Patient voice source on referral/follow-up gaps.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
quote_text_short: “I really tried to get the doctors to listen”
quote_context: Public patient quote in source; context: cancer and other scan-detectable conditions; stage: imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; emergency return
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; red flag missed; repeated visits ignored
pain_point_or_theme: Imaging workflows fail at multiple points: ordering, performance, interpretation, communication, follow-up.
why_it_matters: Closed-loop imaging: critical finding acknowledgment, patient notification and escalation if no action documented.
notes: Patient-complaint/ombudsman evidence with public family quote.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
quote_context: Public clinician/system quote in source; context: gastrointestinal and other specialty referrals; stage: referral; specialist review; imaging/labs ordered; follow-up
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; normal-test trap
pain_point_or_theme: Advice can reduce unnecessary appointments but must not become a soft rejection of unresolved symptoms.
why_it_matters: Record advice decisions with safety-net tasks and escalation criteria.
notes: Includes clinician voice and pathway redesign example.
